metadata.description: A network interface component. device_class covers a physical NIC (PF), a fully virtual interface (virtio / veth), a whole-NIC passthrough to a guest, a partition DERIVED FROM a physical NIC (1→N: SR-IOV VF or VLAN/macvlan sub-interface, parent_device → the physical NIC), and — added 0.2.0 — a composite BUILT FROM multiple interfaces (N→1): an aggregate (bond / LACP LAG, 802.1AX) or a bridge (802.1Q software switch), with lower_layer → its member interfaces. aggregate/bridge are the inverse of partition (many members → one composite vs one parent → many slices), so the whole host interface stack — physical NICs → bond → bridge → sub-interfaces — is one traversable device tree in a single type (common-elements §7). Added 0.3.0: the same type also serves SWITCH PORTS (contained_by Network.Switch), and a physical interface carries a connects_to adjacency (renamed from connected_to, common-elements §9) to its LLDP-discovered peer termination point (host NIC ↔ switch port) — the cross-device edge that makes the estate graph traversable host → NIC → port → switch (common-elements §7a). Mirrors Redfish NetworkAdapter/NetworkPort/NetworkDeviceFunction + RFC 8343 interface stacking (lower-layer-if) + RFC 8345 links + IEEE 802.1AB LLDP.

device_class.description: Device realization (common-elements.md §7). partition = a slice off ONE physical NIC (SR-IOV VF or VLAN/macvlan sub-interface; set parent_device + partition_mechanism). aggregate = a bond / LACP LAG built from MANY member interfaces (set aggregation + lower_layer). bridge = a software L2 bridge over MANY member ports (set bridge + lower_layer). virtual = virtio/veth with no hardware parent. aggregate + bridge are the N→1 inverse of partition's 1→N.

partition_mechanism.description: Only when device_class=partition: e.g. sr-iov, vlan, macvlan, ipvlan.

aggregation.description: Only when device_class=aggregate — bond / link-aggregation parameters (IEEE 802.1AX). The member interfaces are the lower_layer references.

mode.description: Bond mode. 802.3ad = dynamic LACP link aggregation (requires a matching switch LAG); active-backup = failover only. Linux-bonding vocabulary; 802.3ad names IEEE 802.1AX.

lacp_rate.description: Only when mode=802.3ad: LACPDU transmit rate.

miimon.description: MII link-monitoring interval in milliseconds; 0 disables (Linux-bonding vocabulary, like the mode names).

xmit_hash_policy.description: Tx load-balancing hash, e.g. layer2 | layer2+3 | layer3+4 (modes 802.3ad / balance-xor).

min_links.description: Minimum aggregated links for the bond to be considered up.

bridge.description: Only when device_class=bridge — software L2 bridge parameters (IEEE 802.1Q). The bridged ports are the lower_layer references.

vlan_filtering.description: 802.1Q per-port VLAN filtering on the bridge (false = a plain trunk bridge that passes all tags transparently).

location.description: PCIe slot / BDF / port designation within the parent (Redfish PhysicalLocation). Primary discriminator; for a VF/sub-interface, its index/name.

mac_address.description: MAC address (Redfish Ethernet MACAddress) — globally unique interface identity.

role.description: Semantic usage, e.g. mgmt | storage | tenant | trunk.

vlan_id.description: Optional — an OBSERVED/discovered VLAN tag, a discovery convenience only. NOT authoritative: the authoritative membership is `vlan_memberships` (references to Network.VLAN). Discovery may record a raw tag here; resolution promotes it into a vlan_memberships entry.

vlan_memberships.description: The port's VLAN membership set (802.1Q). Each entry REFERENCES an existing Network.VLAN (the shared segment object — the id/encapsulation is pulled from there, adopt-not-absorb) and declares its mode. A trunk carries many (one native/untagged + N tagged); an access port one. Each membership is also a references->Network.VLAN edge.

network_vlan_ref.description: handle/uuid of an existing Network.VLAN this port is a member of.

mode.description: native = the untagged PVID (at most one per port); tagged = a tagged member (802.1Q).

mac_address.description: Realized MAC address (also a discriminator in identity).

relationships.name: host
relationships.description: The host the physical NIC is installed in.

relationships.name: guest
relationships.description: For virtual / passthrough / partition, the guest the interface is presented to.

relationships.name: switch
relationships.description: For a switch port: the switch it belongs to. Same type serves host NICs and switch ports — the §27 Identity block (location = the port designation, e.g. 'Port 14') distinguishes instances.

parent_device — the ONE physical NIC this passthrough/partition (SR-IOV VF, VLAN/macvlan sub-interface) is derived from (1→N). REQUIRED when device_class is passthrough or partition (common-elements §7).
relationships.name: parent_device
relationships.adopted_from: RFC 8343 lower-layer-if (declared refinement: the 1-to-N slice direction)

relationships.description: lower_layer — the MEMBER interfaces this composite is built from (N→1): a bond's aggregated NICs (802.1AX) or a bridge's ports (802.1Q). The inverse of parent_device; RFC 8343 lower-layer-if. REQUIRED when device_class is aggregate or bridge (common-elements §7).
relationships.name: lower_layer

relationships.description: connects_to (renamed from connected_to when relation names were adopted from standards, common-elements §9) — the PEER termination point this physical interface is linked to (host NIC ↔ switch port; switch ↔ switch uplink). Symmetric adjacency, declared once (either end). Discovered via IEEE 802.1AB LLDP (Chassis ID + Port ID name the peer); a mismatch between declared cabling and the LLDP-observed neighbor is drift (OBS-001). RFC 8345 models this as a link between two termination-points — promote to a first-class link entity only if links ever need their own attributes (cable id, patch panel); until then the reference keeps surface area minimal (common-elements §7a).
relationships.name: connects_to
vlan_membership
relationships.description: The VLAN(s) this port is a member of (see spec.vlan_memberships); each carries a mode (native/tagged). The port references the shared Network.VLAN objects rather than duplicating the id.

adopts.role: network-interface component vocabulary (NetworkAdapter/NetworkPort/NetworkDeviceFunction: MAC, speed, MTU)
interface-stacking vocabulary — a composite interface (bond/bridge) references its member interfaces via lower_layer (RFC 8343 lower-layer-if)
software L2 bridge + VLAN vocabulary (device_class=bridge, bridge.vlan_filtering, vlan_id)
relation vocabulary — connects_to peer-adjacency relation name (common-elements §9)

adopts.standard_name: Station and Media Access Control Connectivity Discovery (LLDP)
adopts.role: connects_to peer-discovery vocabulary (host NIC <-> switch port adjacency)
